evidence: Package tree maps cleanly to domain capabilities: api (HTTP layer), config (grammar loading), exploration (semantic field analysis), indexer (corpus ingestion), model (domain types), query (corpus search), utils (shared primitives), viz (visualization)
CLAUDE.md and README.md accurately reflect the code structure: package listings, API endpoints, and command names all match runtime
ExplorationResult and ComparisonResult are documented as traditional classes (not records) with explicit rationale in Javadoc — ownership philosophy is documented

AdjectiveCollocateRanker was extracted from SemanticFieldExplorer 'to keep that class below the 400-line threshold' — this is a size-driven split, not a cohesion-driven split; the extractor is only used by SemanticFieldExplorer and is public rather than package-private
RelationUtils (in api package) contains alias-to-config-ID mapping that is logically config-domain knowledge; it belongs closer to GrammarConfigLoader or RelationConfig
PatternSubstitution (in api package) performs CQL token manipulation; its logic uses CqlUtils and is tightly coupled to the config/query domain, not the HTTP api domain
The viz package contains only RadialPlot.java — this is a thin package that makes the top-level structure look deeper than it is

The model package mixes result DTOs (WordSketchResult, CollocateResult), option bags (ExploreOptions), edge types (RelationEdgeType, Edge), and exploration model objects (DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate) — the package is a catch-all rather than a coherent domain boundary

strengths: Top-level packages map consistently to domain capabilities; a new engineer can understand what each package does from its name alone
Documentation (CLAUDE.md, README.md) is accurate and in sync with the actual code structure
Public surface of each package is small: handler classes are package-private; only the explorer and API server are public-facing
The QueryExecutor interface cleanly defines the query subsystem's contract
high_level_elegance.issue_character: Issues are moderate boundary blurriness — helpers and utilities placed in the api package that belong to the config or query domain, and a model package that is slightly catch-all.
high_level_elegance.score_rationale: The high-level structure is understandable and doc-accurate, which is a genuine strength. However, RelationUtils and PatternSubstitution misplaced in api, and a model package that mixes too many concerns, create minor friction for someone trying to find or change domain logic. This is solid-but-uneven quality — a 74 reflects the good architectural intent undermined by a few placement decisions.

issues.summary: RelationUtils (relation alias mapping) is in the api package but belongs in config
evidence: RelationUtils.RELATION_ALIASES maps short names ('adj_modifier', 'predicate', etc.) to grammar-config IDs ('noun_modifiers', 'noun_adj_predicates', etc.)
This knowledge is about the grammar config domain, not the HTTP api domain
GrammarConfigLoader.getRelation() is the natural neighbour for alias resolution
issues.suggestion: Move RelationUtils into the config package (or fold alias resolution directly into GrammarConfigLoader.getRelation()) so that config knowledge lives in the config layer.

issues.summary: PatternSubstitution (CQL token manipulation) is in the api package but belongs in utils or config
evidence: PatternSubstitution.substituteCollocate() calls CqlUtils.splitCqlTokens() and CqlUtils.extractConstraintAttribute() — it is logically an extension of CqlUtils
The class is package-private to api but its logic (CQL pattern transformation) is domain-neutral and reusable
issues.suggestion: Move PatternSubstitution into the utils package alongside CqlUtils, or merge its single method into CqlUtils as a static utility.

issues.summary: AdjectiveCollocateRanker was extracted from SemanticFieldExplorer for size, not cohesion, creating an artificially split subsystem
evidence: AdjectiveCollocateRanker Javadoc: 'Extracted from SemanticFieldExplorer to keep that class below the 400-line threshold'
AdjectiveCollocateRanker is public but only ever used by SemanticFieldExplorer; it has no independent callers
SemanticFieldExplorer.compareCollocateProfiles is documented as 'an intentional facade over AdjectiveCollocateRanker' — indicating the split is artificial
issues.suggestion: Either make AdjectiveCollocateRanker package-private (since it has no external callers), or re-merge it into SemanticFieldExplorer and accept a longer class. A size heuristic is not a valid reason for a public type.

root_causes: api package used as a convenience location for helpers that don't have an obvious home
model package grew incrementally without periodic reshaping of its responsibility boundary
likely_symptoms: new engineers adding config-domain logic to api package following the existing pattern of RelationUtils
